preamble: Debuccalization of the voiceless postalveolar fricative [ʃ] to the glottal fricative [h], typically involving a loss of oral articulation.

examples.note: Historical shift of sibilants to glottal fricatives (e.g. Proto-Indo-European *septm → Greek hepta).
Aspiration of sibilants in syllable-final position or as a general dialectal trait.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
